"'''Hoth'''" is a two-page [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] featured in [[Star Wars Tales 5|''Star Wars Tales'' #5]], published by [[Dark Horse Comics]] on [[September 27]], [[2000]]. It was written and penciled by [[Tony Millionaire]], colored by [[Michelle Madsen]], and edited by [[Philip Amara|Phil Amara]].

The small comic tells the story of a [[Wampa/Legends|wampa]] that stumbles upon an unsuspecting [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Viper probe droid/Legends|viper probe droid]] while hunting [[Tauntaun/Legends|tauntauns]] on the ice [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]].

==Plot summary==
[[File:Probedroid_upside-down.jpg|thumb|180px|The captured [[Viper probe droid/Legends|Viper probe droid]] hangs in the [[Wampa/Legends|wampa's]] ice cave.]]
A hungry [[Wampa/Legends|wampa]] is stalking a herd of at least five [[Tauntaun/Legends|tauntauns]] grazing on [[Lichen/Legends|lichen]] near one of [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth's]] frozen lakes. The wampa prepares itself for an attack, and then pounces, trying to capture one of the tauntauns. However, it runs into an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Viper probe droid/Legends|probe droid]] by accident, and after a brief battle of [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]] versus claws, the wampa defeats the [[Probe droid/Legends|probe droid]] and brings it back to its cave. The [[Droid/Legends|droid]] later finds itself hanging upside-down in the ice cave, above the skeletal remains of the wampa's former prey.

==Development==
"Hoth" first appeared in [[Dark Horse Comics|Dark Horse Comic's]] [[Star Wars Tales 5|''Star Wars Tales'' 5]], released on [[September 27]], [[2000]]. It was later collected in ''[[Star Wars Tales Volume 2]]'', which was published on [[May 1]], [[2002]]. The two-page [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] was written and penciled by [[Tony Millionaire]] as his first foray into the ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[Star Wars Legends|Expanded Universe]], colored by [[Michelle Madsen]], and edited by [[Philip Amara|Phil Amara]].

The way the wampa hangs the probe droid in the cave at the end of the comic is reminiscent of the method with which the wampa imprisons [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] in [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']].

==Continuity==
Because "Hoth" was published in an issue of ''Star Wars Tales'' before [[Star Wars Tales 21|issue 21]], the [[canon]]icity of the story was originally ambiguous.<ref name="Chee">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=752#14952461|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Leland Y Chee]]|date=2006-06-04|time=3:05 AM|quote=Consider everything that's not completely outrageous or intentionally comic as S-canon. If it's referenced in another non-Tales source, then elevate it to C-canon.}}</ref> However, the events of "Hoth" were later referenced and expanded in [[The Official Star Wars Fact File 51|''The Official Star Wars Fact File'' 51]], making the story fully canonical. According to the ''Fact File'', scientists from the [[University of Sanbra]] later discovered the probe droid in the wampa's cave and used the recordings from its memory to learn about the behavior of the ice creatures.
